actionUnits.condition: GameObject has a BoxCollider (enabled, non-trigger) and no Rigidbody; no EventTrigger component. Attached MonoBehaviour (MonoBehaviour_0) maps exactly to Fire.cs (m_Script.fileID=11500000, guid=1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d, type=3). Scene-configured fields: isOn=false, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, maxNeighborDistance=4, downMultiplier=2, totalHitTime=5, onNeighborsThreshold=2, minNeighborDownDistance=3, mainEffectGo.fileID=1849464941111748095. Fire exposes only parameterized public methods (SetIsOn(bool), WaterHit(Vector3), UpdateFireState(bool), NotifyNeighborState(bool), FindNeighbors(Fire[], int)); there are no UnityEvents (m_Calls) to invoke and no public parameterless methods that can be triggered directly. To fully test behavior, external systems must invoke WaterHit(normal) (via IWaterInteractable) and neighbor fires must change state to drive CheckNeighborState/Update() branches.
